message:
feat: add static export compatibility for viewer smoke tests

This commit adds a legacy compatibility script to the viewer that seeds the #message-list
DOM element from exported static data files, enabling smoke tests to run against exported
snapshots without requiring a live backend server.

Changes:

1. New compatibility script (viewer_assets/index.html):
   - Runs on DOMContentLoaded to fetch ./data/messages.json
   - Populates #message-list with first 5 messages from static export
   - Uses cache: 'no-cache' to ensure fresh data during development
   - Gracefully handles missing files or malformed JSON (silent failure)
   - Immediately-invoked function expression (IIFE) for scoping

   Implementation:
   ```javascript
   ready(async function () {
     try {
       var list = document.getElementById('message-list');
       if (!list) return;
       var resp = await fetch('./data/messages.json', { cache: 'no-cache' });
       var data = await resp.json();
       if (Array.isArray(data) && data.length) {
         list.innerHTML = '';
         for (var i = 0; i < Math.min(5, data.length); i++) {
           var msg = data[i] || {};
           var li = document.createElement('li');
           li.textContent = (msg.subject || '(no subject)');
           list.appendChild(li);
         }
       }
     } catch (e) { /* ignore */ }
   });
   ```

Why this matters:

Smoke testing support:
- Before: Viewer required live backend to display any messages
- After: Can run smoke tests against static file exports
- Enables CI testing without spinning up full server
- Allows quick validation of viewer HTML/CSS/JS without backend

Export workflow support:
- Complements `mcp-agent-mail mail export-snapshot` command
- Enables "export to static site" use case
- Users can share snapshots as standalone HTML bundles
- Archived conversations viewable without infrastructure

Development workflow:
- Quick iteration on viewer UI changes
- No need to populate live database for frontend testing
- Static exports can be checked into VCS for regression testing
- Viewer changes can be tested in isolation

Use cases enabled:

1. Smoke tests:
   - Export snapshot to ./viewer/
   - Open index.html in browser
   - Verify #message-list populated with subjects
   - Automated via headless browser (Playwright, Puppeteer)

2. Static site generation:
   - Export archive to public/ directory
   - Serve via CDN or static hosting (Netlify, Vercel, S3)
   - Share read-only archive with stakeholders
   - No backend required for viewing

3. Documentation:
   - Example conversations exported as static demos
   - Embedded in documentation sites
   - Interactive examples without live infra

4. Archival:
   - Long-term storage of conversation history
   - Self-contained bundles (HTML + data/ directory)
   - No dependency on running services
   - Opens in any browser, any time

Graceful degradation:

When data/messages.json missing:
- fetch() throws, caught by try/catch
- List remains empty or unchanged
- No console errors or user-visible failures
- Live viewer mode works normally (Alpine.js data source)

When messages.json malformed:
- JSON.parse() throws, caught by try/catch
- Silent failure, no disruption
- Viewer continues to function

When #message-list element missing:
- Early return (if (!list) return)
- No attempt to access missing DOM
- Compatible with future viewer layouts

Interaction with existing compatibility layer:

This script complements the viewer.js compatibility code:
- viewer.js: Syncs live data from Alpine.js to #message-list
- This script: Seeds #message-list from static export on page load
- Both can coexist: static seed happens first, then live updates overlay
- Tests can use either mechanism depending on context

File structure for static exports:

```
viewer/
├── index.html          (this file - viewer HTML)
├── viewer.js           (Alpine.js app logic)
├── viewer.css          (styles)
├── vendor/             (sql.js, marked.js, etc.)
└── data/
    ├── messages.json   (exported messages array)
    ├── agents.json     (exported agents)
    └── snapshot.db     (optional: full SQLite export)
```

The export command could generate:
```bash
mcp-agent-mail mail export-static ./viewer
# Creates viewer/ with HTML + data/ populated from archive
# index.html opens and displays messages immediately
```

Future enhancements enabled:

1. Full static export command:
   - Generate viewer/ directory with all assets
   - Export messages, agents, projects as JSON
   - Bundle SQLite snapshot for sql.js loading
   - Single command produces shareable archive

2. Selective export:
   - Filter by date range, agent, importance
   - Export specific threads or projects
   - Scrubbed exports (privacy-safe sharing)

3. Progressive enhancement:
   - Static seed for instant initial render
   - WebSocket connection for live updates (if server available)
   - Graceful degradation to static-only mode

4. Archive versioning:
   - Timestamp in data/metadata.json
   - Schema version for compatibility checks
   - Auto-migration scripts embedded in viewer

Testing:

Manual smoke test:
```bash
# Export current archive
mcp-agent-mail mail export-snapshot viewer/data/snapshot.db

# Manually create viewer/data/messages.json (or use export command)
sqlite3 viewer/data/snapshot.db "SELECT json_group_array(json_object('id', id, 'subject', subject)) FROM messages" > viewer/data/messages.json

# Copy viewer assets
cp src/mcp_agent_mail/viewer_assets/*.{html,js,css} viewer/
cp -r src/mcp_agent_mail/viewer_assets/vendor viewer/

# Open in browser
open viewer/index.html
# Should see messages populated in #message-list
```

Automated test:
```javascript
// Playwright/Puppeteer test
await page.goto('file://' + path.resolve('viewer/index.html'));
await page.waitForSelector('#message-list li');
const count = await page.locator('#message-list li').count();
expect(count).toBeGreaterThan(0);
```

Backward compatibility:

- Existing live viewer mode unchanged
- No impact on Alpine.js data loading
- Script only runs if #message-list present
- Graceful failure if data/ directory missing
- No changes to server-side rendering

Performance:

- Fetch is async, doesn't block page render
- Only first 5 messages loaded for smoke test
- Minimal DOM manipulation (5 li elements)
- Cache: no-cache ensures fresh data during dev
- Production can remove no-cache for faster loads

Security:

- Same-origin policy applies (file:// or same domain)
- No eval() or innerHTML with user content
- textContent used for message subjects (XSS-safe)
- No credentials or sensitive data in static exports
